LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ARE RAISED IN THE 1ST THROUGH 16TH CAUSES OF ACTION. BURDEN OF PROOF IS NOT SHIFTED AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION. ISSUE RAISED AS TO ALTER EGO DUE TO KAPLAN'S USE OF PERSONAL EXPENSES ON INTOUCH CREDIT CARD(ROSS DEPOSITION ). ISSUE RAISED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OFA CTION (FRAUD AND VIOLATION OF CORP. CODE SECTION 25401) WHERE EMAIL THAT ACCOMPANIED THE P.P.M. STATED IT WAS THE FINAL AND COMPLETE REPRENSATION OF INVESTORS, BUT THE SEPT. P.P.M CONTAINED TERMS THAT WERE MATERIALLY CHANGED EVIDENCING A POSSIBBLE MISREPRESENTATION . AS TO 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION(CONVERSION AND MONEY HAD AND RECEIVED) THIEROT TESTIFIED SHE LOANED MONEY TO INTOUCH AND DID NOT INVESTIT. THIERIOT DECLARATION PARAGRAPHS 15-17) AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION(BREACH OF CONTRACT0 THIERIOT TESTIFIED THAT SHE PERFORMED UNDER THE AGRGEEMENT AND WAS NOT PAID. AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION(UNLAWFUL FRAUDULENT TRANSFER ACT) ISSUE RAISED WHETHER THE $500,000 WAS A LOAN TO "BEEF UP" THE BALANCE SHEET AS THIERIOT ALLEGES, WHICH WOULD NOT ENTITLE CROSS DEFENDANTS TO TRANSFER THIS MONEY TO KAPLAN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER)(302/CWW) JUDGE CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER KENT GUBINNE, CSR # 5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT ELISABETH THIERIOT (INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE THIEROT REVOCABLE TRUST) AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS LESTER PETRACCA AND CHRISTOPHER SLOWINSKI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT OBTAINS A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT

CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY.

APPEARANCES: Richard F. Munzinger, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP 773-7340 for moving parties (possible confusion over tentative ruling notice issue); Karen Douglas, paralegal to E. Craig Moody, Esq. 951-9900 atty for opposing parties.

Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-01-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT INTOUCH GROUP, INC. AND CROSS-DEFENDANT JOSHUA KAPLAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Richard F. Munzinger, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP 773-7340 for opposing parties; Karen Douglas, paralegal to E. Craig Moody, Esq. 951-9900 atty for moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Summary judgment and summary adjudication denied. Triable issues of fact are raised in the 1st through 6th causes of action, burden of proof not shifted as to the 7th cause of action. Issue raised as to alter ego due to Kaplan's use of personal expenses on Intouch credit card (Ross deposition). Issue raised as to 1st and 2nd causes of action (fraud and violation of Corporations Code section 25401) where the email that accompanied the May P.P.M. stated it was the final and complete presentation to investors, but the September P.P.M. contained terms that were materially changed evidencing a possible misrepresentation. As to 3rd and 4th causes of action (conversion and money had and received) Thieriot testified she loaned money to Intouch And did not invest it. )Thieriot declaration paragraphs 15-17). As to the 5th cause of action (breach of contract) Thieriot testified that she performed under the agreement and was not paid. As to 6th cause of action (unlawful fraudulent transfer act) issue raised whether the $500,000 was a loan to "beef up" the balance sheet as Thieriot alleges, which would not entitle cross defendants to transfer this money to Kaplan. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
